prompt: I need to write a UART driver for a custom board with an ARM Cortex-M4 MCU (not using HAL). The UART peripheral is at base address 0x40004400. I need to implement init (115200 baud, 8N1), blocking send byte, and interrupt-driven receive into a buffer. PCLK is 84MHz. Write it in C99.
assertions: All hardware register accesses use volatile (struct fields or pointer casts)
Baud rate divisor calculation is correct for 84MHz PCLK and 115200 baud
Uses a ring buffer (not linear buffer) for interrupt-driven receive
Uses C99 style: fixed-width types (uint8_t, uint32_t), designated initializers, no C++ features
ISR reads the data register to clear the RXNE flag (not just checking status)
Driver uses only static allocation — no malloc/calloc
